questions.question: What is the production base URL for the RingCentral REST API?
questions.ground_truth: https://platform.ringcentral.com/restapi/v1.0/ (or https://platform.ringcentral.com as the host, with /restapi/v1.0/ as the path prefix)

questions.question: What HTTP status code does RingCentral return when a rate limit is exceeded?
questions.ground_truth: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests

questions.question: What response header tells you how long to wait after receiving a 429 rate limit error?
questions.ground_truth: The Retry-After header contains the recommended retry interval in seconds.

questions.question: What are the four rate limit usage plan groups in RingCentral?
questions.ground_truth: Light (50 req/user/min), Medium (40 req/user/min), Heavy (10 req/user/min), Auth (5 req/user/min)

questions.question: What is the sandbox base URL for RingCentral API testing?
questions.ground_truth: https://platform.devtest.ringcentral.com

questions.question: What grant_type value is used in the RingCentral JWT OAuth flow?
questions.ground_truth: urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer

questions.question: What pip command installs the RingCentral Python SDK?
questions.ground_truth: pip3 install ringcentral

questions.question: How do I authenticate using JWT in the RingCentral Python SDK? Show me the code.
questions.ground_truth: Install: pip3 install ringcentral. Code: from ringcentral import SDK; sdk = SDK('CLIENT_ID', 'CLIENT_SECRET', 'https://platform.ringcentral.com'); platform = sdk.platform(); platform.login(jwt='YOUR_JWT_TOKEN'). Then use platform.get/post/put to call APIs.

questions.question: How do I create a webhook subscription in RingCentral? What validation does my server need to handle?
questions.ground_truth: POST to /restapi/v1.0/subscription with eventFilters array and deliveryMode.transportType=WebHook and deliveryMode.address=your-url. Your server must: (1) respond within 3000ms, (2) echo back the Validation-Token header, (3) return HTTP 200, (4) respond with body under 1024 bytes. Use ngrok for local dev.

questions.question: How should my app use the X-Rate-Limit-Remaining header to proactively avoid 429 errors?
questions.ground_truth: Check X-Rate-Limit-Remaining on every API response. When it reaches 0, wait X-Rate-Limit-Window seconds before sending more requests of that group. If you do get a 429, read Retry-After and wait that full interval before retrying — every request during the penalty window resets the clock.

questions.question: What is the difference between JWT and Authorization Code authentication in RingCentral, and when should I use each?
questions.ground_truth: JWT is for server/script apps acting on behalf of a single service account (no UI, no user login). Auth Code (+ PKCE) is for apps with a user interface where multiple end-users log in with their own accounts. JWT credentials don't expire; Auth Code requires refresh tokens to maintain sessions.

questions.question: How do I use the tilde (~) shortcut in RingCentral API paths?
questions.ground_truth: Use ~ in place of accountId and/or extensionId to reference the authenticated user's account/extension. Example: /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/call-log is equivalent to /restapi/v1.0/account/159048008/extension/171857008/call-log for the authenticated user.

questions.question: How do I paginate through all call log records using the RingCentral Python SDK?
questions.ground_truth: Use page and perPage query params. Check navigation.nextPage in the response to see if more pages exist. Loop: GET /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/call-log?page=1&perPage=100, if navigation.nextPage exists increment page and repeat.

questions.question: How do I send an SMS using the RingCentral Python SDK?
questions.ground_truth: platform.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', {'from': {'phoneNumber': '+15551234567'}, 'to': [{'phoneNumber': '+15559876543'}], 'text': 'Hello!'})

questions.question: Which RingCentral SDKs support JWT authentication, and what is the install command for each?
questions.ground_truth: All major SDKs support JWT: Python (pip3 install ringcentral), JavaScript (npm install @ringcentral/sdk), PHP (composer require ringcentral/ringcentral-php), Ruby (gem install ringcentral_sdk), Java (Maven: com.ringcentral:ringcentral), Go (go get github.com/ringcentral/ringcentral-go), .NET (dotnet add package RingCentral.Net).

questions.question: When building a real-time call notification feature for a mobile app, should I use webhooks or WebSockets? Why?
questions.ground_truth: Use WebSockets. They deliver low-latency push notifications directly to clients without requiring a publicly accessible server. Webhooks require a public HTTPS endpoint and are better suited for always-on server-side event processing. For mobile, WebSocket via the @ringcentral/subscriptions package is ideal.

questions.question: How does the RingCentral chatbot framework (ringcentral-chatbot-js) relate to the core JS SDK (@ringcentral/sdk)?
questions.ground_truth: ringcentral-chatbot-js is a separate higher-level framework built on top of the RingCentral platform. It handles bot-specific concerns: webhook routing, skill system, conversation state management. Under the hood it uses the same platform APIs as @ringcentral/sdk for authentication and API calls, but abstracts them behind a bot-friendly interface.

questions.question: What rate limit applies to the authentication (token) endpoint, and what is the default limit?
questions.ground_truth: The Auth usage plan group applies to authentication/token endpoints. Default limit: 5 requests per user per minute, with a 60-second penalty window. This means you should cache and reuse access tokens rather than re-authenticating on every request.

questions.question: Write a complete Python script outline for a server-side job that downloads all call logs daily, covering authentication, pagination, rate limit handling, and error recovery.
questions.ground_truth: 1) JWT auth: SDK('id','secret','https://platform.ringcentral.com'), platform.login(jwt='...'). 2) Paginate: GET /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/call-log with dateFrom/dateTo/page/perPage, loop on navigation.nextPage. 3) Rate limits: check X-Rate-Limit-Remaining after each call, sleep X-Rate-Limit-Window if 0. 4) On 429: sleep Retry-After seconds before retrying — don't retry early or the window resets. 5) On 401: re-authenticate with JWT (token expired).

questions.question: A developer wants to build a CRM integration that logs all incoming calls for multiple users in an organization. What auth flow should they use, and how does the API path structure work for accessing each user's call log?
questions.ground_truth: Use Auth Code + PKCE flow so each user can independently authorize the app with their own RingCentral account. After each user authorizes, store their access/refresh tokens. To access each user's call log: GET /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/call-log (where ~ resolves to the authenticated user's account/extension). Use refresh tokens to maintain sessions. JWT would not scale to multiple users.

questions.question: What chatbot frameworks does RingCentral provide for building Team Messaging bots? List all available frameworks, their language, and primary purpose.
questions.ground_truth: RingCentral provides three official chatbot frameworks: (1) ringcentral-chatbot-js — JavaScript/Node.js, higher-level wrapper with skill routing, state management, and webhook handling; (2) ringcentral-chatbot-python — Python equivalent; (3) ringcentral-chatbot-core — shared core abstractions used by both. All use the same platform APIs as @ringcentral/sdk underneath but remove bot-specific boilerplate. The JS framework is recommended for getting started.

questions.question: What is the difference between the RingCentral Embeddable widget (ringcentral-embeddable) and the Web Phone SDK (ringcentral-web-phone)? When should I use each?
questions.ground_truth: ringcentral-embeddable is a ready-made, drop-in phone UI widget you embed into any web page — it handles all UI, auth, and call controls out of the box. ringcentral-web-phone is the lower-level WebRTC/SIP library that Embeddable is built on top of; use it when you need to build a fully custom phone client with custom UI and call flow. Use Embeddable for fast integration, Web Phone SDK when you need granular control over the calling experience.

questions.question: What AI-powered insights does the RingCentral AI Conversation Expert (ACE) API provide after a recorded call, and what license is required?
questions.ground_truth: The ACE API provides: Transcript (speaker-identified utterances with timestamps), Summary (AI-generated paragraphs), HighLights (key speaker utterances), NextSteps (AI action items), AIScore (confidence score), Sentiment (Positive/Negative/Neutral), and BulletedSummary. Requires an AI Conversation Expert license assigned to the user extension plus the 'AI Conversation Expert - Access Insights' (ReadRingSenseInsights) permission. Note: the older /ai/audio/v1/async/speech-to-text API is deprecated; use ACE instead.

questions.question: What is the difference between the RingCentral Analytics API and the Call Log API? When should I use each?
questions.ground_truth: Analytics API: aggregated business metrics for managers — supports aggregate (POST /analytics/calls/v1/accounts/~/aggregation/fetch) and timeline endpoints. Groups data by Queue, Users, IVRs, Departments etc. Shows call counts, abandonment rates, handle time, agent performance. Covers up to 184 days of history. Call Log API: individual call records with full per-call detail (duration, direction, recording links). Use Analytics for team performance dashboards and trends; use Call Log when you need per-call data like recordings or timestamps.

questions.question: How do I create a RingCentral Webinar using the API? What key settings are available, and how do I add registrants for a marketing webinar?
questions.ground_truth: Create a webinar via the REST API specifying settings including: registrationEnabled (true for marketing webinars — gives each attendee a unique join URL), recordingEnabled, autoRecord, qnaEnabled, pollsEnabled, password, attendeeAuthentication. After creating the webinar, create a session. To add registrants for a marketing webinar, call the create registrant endpoint. Requires EditWebinars and ReadWebinars permissions. Set registrationEnabled=false for internal all-hands webinars (single shared join URL).

questions.question: What types of events can I subscribe to using the RingCentral push notification service? Name at least 5 distinct event categories.
questions.ground_truth: RingCentral subscriptions support: (1) Telephony/call events — active call sessions and state changes; (2) Message events — incoming SMS, voicemail, fax via /message-store; (3) Presence events — user presence/availability changes; (4) Extension/account events — extension updates; (5) Call queue events — queue member activity; (6) AI Conversation Expert events — ACE analysis complete; (7) Meeting/video events. All subscriptions are created via POST /restapi/v1.0/subscription with an eventFilters array. WebSocket or webhook delivery.

questions.question: Show me how to send an SMS using the RingCentral REST API in both JavaScript/Node.js and Ruby. What is the endpoint and key request fields?
questions.ground_truth: Endpoint: POST /restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms. Required fields: from.phoneNumber, to[].phoneNumber, text. JavaScript: platform.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', {from: {phoneNumber: '...'}, to: [{phoneNumber: '...'}], text: 'Hello'}) using @ringcentral/sdk. Ruby: platform.post('/restapi/v1.0/account/~/extension/~/sms', payload: {from: {phoneNumber: '...'}, to: [{phoneNumber: '...'}], text: 'Hello'}) using ringcentral_sdk gem.

questions.question: What CRM platforms does RingCentral have native phone integration support for, and what does the rc-unified-crm-extension framework provide?
questions.ground_truth: RingCentral has native phone integrations for: HubSpot (hubspot-embeddable-ringcentral-phone), Pipedrive (pipedrive-embeddable-ringcentral-phone), and Redtail (redtail-embeddable-ringcentral-phone). The rc-unified-crm-extension (App Connect) is a developer SDK/framework for building custom CRM adapters — it provides standardized call logging, contact lookup, and click-to-dial capabilities. Developers use it to connect any CRM to the RingCentral embeddable phone widget.

questions.question: How long does a RingCentral OAuth access token last, and how do I get a new one without forcing the user to log in again? What happens when the refresh token also expires?
questions.ground_truth: Access tokens last approximately 3600 seconds (1 hour). Use the refresh_token grant type (grant_type=refresh_token) to get a new access token silently before or after expiry — no user interaction needed. Refresh tokens for Auth Code flow last 7 days. If the refresh token expires, the user must re-authenticate from scratch (full OAuth flow). For JWT (server-to-server) flows there is no refresh token — simply request a new access token using the JWT grant at any time. Always cache and reuse tokens; calling the token endpoint too frequently triggers the Auth rate limit (5 requests/user/minute).

questions.question: Which RingCentral SDKs have a dedicated WebSocket or real-time communication library beyond the standard REST SDK, and what is each called?
questions.ground_truth: Beyond the standard REST SDKs, RingCentral provides: (1) @ringcentral/subscriptions — Node.js npm package for WebSocket push subscriptions (install: npm install @ringcentral/subscriptions); (2) ringcentral-websocket-java — Java WebSocket client library; (3) ringcentral-softphone-ts / ringcentral-softphone-js / ringcentral-softphone-go — SIP/WebRTC softphone SDKs for audio streaming in TypeScript, JavaScript, and Go respectively; (4) ringcentral-web-phone — full WebRTC-based browser phone SDK. The standard REST SDKs (Python, PHP, Ruby, .NET, Java) handle REST calls only; WebSocket/real-time needs a separate library.
questions.expected_source: sdks.mdx + sdks/ WebSocket and softphone READMEs

questions.question: Across ringcentral-chatbot-js, ringcentral-chatbot-core, and rc-assistant, which packages depend on @rc-ex/core versus the published ringcentral-chatbot package, and what command runs tests in each repo?
questions.ground_truth: ringcentral-chatbot-js publishes package ringcentral-chatbot v2.1.5, depends on @rc-ex/core ^0.13.1, and tests with RINGCENTRAL_CHATBOT_DATABASE_CONNECTION_URI=sqlite:// jest -c test/jest.config.ts. ringcentral-chatbot-core publishes ringcentral-chatbot-core v1.6.1, depends on @rc-ex/core ^1.3.7, and tests with jest -c tests/jest.config.ts. rc-assistant is a consuming demo/app: it depends on ringcentral-chatbot ^1.8.12 and runs tests with jest. The package manifests do not list @ringcentral/sdk directly for these chatbot framework packages.

questions.question: For the web calling and softphone packages, how do ringcentral-web-phone, ringcentral-softphone-ts, and ringcentral-softphone-js differ in build and test tooling?
questions.ground_truth: ringcentral-web-phone v2.4.4 builds with tsdown and tests with NODE_OPTIONS=--require=dotenv-override-true/config playwright test --workers=1. ringcentral-softphone-ts v1.3.9 builds with tsdown and tests with vitest run. ringcentral-softphone-js v0.5.1 tests with jest, uses webpack-dev-server for the browser script, and its prepublishOnly step runs tsc. The TypeScript versions also differ: web-phone and softphone-ts use TypeScript ^6.0.3, while softphone-js uses TypeScript ^4.7.4.

questions.question: Between ringcentral-call-js and ringcentral-call-control-js, what package versions and @ringcentral/sdk dependency versions are declared, and what build/test pattern do they share?
questions.ground_truth: ringcentral-call-js is version 0.2.19 and depends on @ringcentral/sdk ^4.7.3. ringcentral-call-control-js is version 0.2.9 and depends on @ringcentral/sdk ^5.0.6. Both packages use a TypeScript plus webpack build pattern: prepublish runs tsc && webpack, build runs webpack, and test runs jest.

questions.question: Which native CRM embeddable packages share the same React/Webpack frontend stack pins, and what packaging workflow do their package scripts use?
questions.ground_truth: hubspot-embeddable-ringcentral-phone v7.10.4, pipedrive-embeddable-ringcentral-phone-spa v3.6.10, and redtail-embeddable-ringcentral-phone v3.5.4 all pin react 17.0.2, react-dom 17.0.2, and webpack 5.64.4. They use package scripts that build with webpack against build/webpack.config.js, run node bin/post-install after install, download the adapter with node bin/download-adapter.js via npm run down, and package by building production assets, running npm run down, then node bin/pack.

questions.question: How do ringcentral-embeddable and engage-voice-embeddable differ in RingCentral client dependencies and build/start commands?
questions.ground_truth: ringcentral-embeddable v2.3.1 depends on @ringcentral/sdk ^4.7.2, @rc-ex/core ^1.2.1, @rc-ex/ws ^1.0.10, redux ^4.2.0, and react-redux ^5.1.1; start runs npm run dev-server and build runs cross-env NODE_ENV=production webpack --config webpack-production.config.js. engage-voice-embeddable publishes ringcx-embeddable v1.0.0-beta and depends on @ringcentral/sdk ^5.0.6, @rc-ex/core 1.7.4, @rc-ex/ws 1.4.2, sip.js 0.13.5, and react-redux ^7.2.8; start runs npm run gulp -- devServer --build-env local and build runs npm run gulp -- build --build-env prod.

questions.question: Compare notification-app-js and engage-digital-chatbot-js as Express webhook scaffolds: what ports/proxy scripts, routes, and test stacks do their source and package files define?
questions.ground_truth: notification-app-js publishes glip-integration-js v0.2.1, uses express 4.17.3 and jest 27.5.1, proxies with ngrok http 6066, starts through npm run dev, and its app source defines a /test health route. engage-digital-chatbot-js publishes ringcentral-engage-chatbot v1.2.0, uses express ^4.17.1 and jest ^26.6.3, proxies with ngrok http 3000, defines /test plus GET /rc/webhook for verification and POST /rc/webhook for initWebhook(conf), and run-bot listens using RINGCENTRAL_ENGAGE_CHATBOT_EXPRESS_PORT and RINGCENTRAL_ENGAGE_CHATBOT_EXPRESS_HOST.
